title: Localization support for web app manifests
description: Your manifest can now support multiple languages.

title: Unlock Structured Clone for Chrome Extension Messaging
description: Chrome extension developers can now opt-in to use the Structured Clone algorithm for message serialization.

title: What's New in WebGPU (Chrome 147-148)
description: WGSL linear_indexing extension and WebGPU on Linux NVIDIA.

title: Final Soft Navigations origin trial starting in Chrome 147
description: Take a last chance to try out the Soft Navigations API before release with this final origin trial.

title: Connection Allowlists origin trial: Secure your web application's network
description: Join the origin trial for Connection Allowlists, a security mechanism in Chrome that creates a network sandbox for documents and web workers.

title: Improved Japanese phonetic name support in Chrome autofill
description: Learn how Chrome Autofill is improving support for Japanese phonetic names (Furigana), making it easier for users to fill out web forms.

title: Take our course about AI evaluations
description: Over the next several weeks, we'll release lessons on AI evals.

title: Chrome Web Store: A smarter, faster appeals process
description: We're launching a new appeals process in the Chrome Web Store.

title: Chrome 148 beta
description: Get a preview of the next Chrome release with this post detailing the features in the current beta.

title: New in Chrome 147
description: Element-scoped view transitions, CSS contrast-color(), and the border-shape property.
